blurb: Apollo Research, an AI-safety evaluations organisation (London & SF) focused on detecting deceptive/scheming behaviour in frontier AI and on third-party model audits.

hiring.note: Lever board checked 2026-07-02; existing governance EOI entry retitled to current first-party posting title.

publications.title: Stress-Testing Deliberative Alignment for Anti-Scheming Training
publications.summary: Tests whether deliberative-alignment training reduces scheming behaviour in frontier models and where it breaks down under stress.

publications.title: Chain-of-Thought Monitorability: A New and Fragile Opportunity for AI Safety
publications.summary: Argues chain-of-thought offers a valuable but fragile window for monitoring AI reasoning, and urges preserving it as models scale.

publications.title: More Capable Models Are Better at In-Context Scheming
publications.summary: Finds that more capable frontier models exhibit stronger in-context scheming, a scaling trend relevant to AI governance.

notes: Publications harvested from apolloresearch.ai/science (real datePublished JSON-LD per page; sitemap lastmod was a bulk-migration timestamp and was NOT used as date). News from /blog/ with real datePublished. Existing hiring/roles preserved (not clobbered). No legal/regulatory action against Apollo found; controversies empty.

news.title: Apollo Update (May 2026)
news.summary: Organisational update on Apollo Research's work and direction.

news.title: Apollo Research is becoming a PBC
news.summary: Apollo announces its transition to a public-benefit corporation structure.
